headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Graphic: Track U.S., state and county gas prices
headlines.description: Are gas prices declining? NBC News is tracking national, state and county prices at the pump.

headlines.title: Map: See how gas prices have changed in the county where you live
headlines.description: How much gas prices have increased during the Iran war depends entirely on where you live, an NBC News analysis of average gas price data from AAA shows.

headlines.title: Trump says ‘I love the inflation’ as annual rate jumps to a 3-year high
headlines.description: The president suggested the recent numbers mean there will be a bigger decline in inflation once the war in Iran is over.

headlines.title: Trump Says 'I Love the Inflation' as Consumer Price Index Rises
headlines.description: After newly-released federal data showed inflation spiking to its highest level in three years, driven in large part by the soaring gas and oil prices related to the United States’ war with Iran, President Donald Trump reacted saying, “I love the inflation.” He later said that what he meant to say is that “despite the fact that we’re in a war, the numbers are much lower than anticipated.” NBC’s Garrett Haake reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: CPI report: Inflation rises to 4.2%, the highest levels in three years
headlines.description: May inflation rose to 4.2% year over year, the highest level in three years, according to the Consumer Price Index report. NBC News’ Christine Romans reports on prices rises by sectors and how wages haven’t been keeping up with costs.

headlines.title: Tips to Build Wealth: Saving Pre-Taxed Income, Investing, More
headlines.description: David Bach, bestselling author of “The Automatic Millionaire,” joins TODAY’s Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones to smart money moves you can make at any age. “The next 10 years are what I believe will be the greatest opportunity to build wealth in our lifetime. We have never seen what’s about to happen and that’s because of AI,” he says. Tips include a formula to save some of your pre-taxed income, new retirement accounts for children and more.

headlines.title: Creative Ways to Cut Down Expenses and Save Some Extra Cash
headlines.description: Looking for ways to cut down on some expenses? Codie Sanchez, author of "Main Street Millionaire" and founder of Contrarian Thinking, joins TODAY's Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones with ways to save some extra cash. Tips include negotiating on everything from streaming services to gym memberships, being open and vocal about your budgeting goals, investing in essential businesses and more.

headlines.title: How shoppers who pay in cash are subsidizing Americans’ credit card reward points
headlines.description: A Harvard study estimates that people who pay with cash and debit cards are subsidizing $30 billion a year in points and perks for credit card users.

headlines.title: Trump downplays inflation concerns as rate hits highest level since early 2023
headlines.description: Consumers and U.S. workers are feeling the pinch of a wage growth rate that lags behind the rate of inflation.

headlines.title: Paramount accuses Netflix of ‘scorched-earth’ campaign against Warner Bros. deal
headlines.description: Paramount accused Netflix of launching a “scorched-earth campaign to try and poison regulators and other stakeholders” against its purchase of Warner Bros.

headlines.title: Kalshi looks to tackle insider trading by asking some users for their employer info
headlines.description: Kalshi will soon require users to disclose their employers to place certain trades that involve sensitive information.
